name: Coda
description: Coda is an all-in-one collaborative workspace that brings teams, tools, and workflows together in one doc so teams can make docs, build workflows, and move work forward.

auth_methods.note: Official docs describe Personal API tokens used as Bearer tokens (Authorization: Bearer <token>) and OAuth 2.0 for integrations.

credential_access.note: Docs state the Coda API is available to all users free of charge (both free and paid workspaces) and personal API tokens can be created in account settings (https://coda.io/account/settings/apis).

api_surface.note: The v1beta1 reference documents a full REST API (base path https://coda.io/apis/v1) covering docs, pages, folders, tables, rows, formulas, controls, automations, analytics, etc., and an OpenAPI spec is provided.

buildability.note: Public REST docs + an ability to create personal API tokens via account settings (self-serve) mean a developer can obtain credentials and use the API today. See the API docs and account settings pages for token creation.

queries: Coda Developers apis v1beta1 authentication create token site:coda.io
